channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sandiganbayan issues arrest warrant vs Jinggoy Estrada over plunder case
item.description: Former Philippine senator Jinggoy Estrada is facing a new arrest warrant issued in connection with a plunder case classified as non-bailable, according to a report from ABS-CBN News. The case is being...

item.title: Ethiopia holds nationwide election amid conflict as Ebola outbreak response and regional security concerns dominate Africa news
item.description: Across Africa, major developments have been reported on June 1, 2026, highlighting political, health, and security challenges across the continent. In Ethiopia, millions of voters are heading to the p...

item.title: Lesotho expands clean energy access, reaching 147,000 people in rural electrification drive
item.description: Lesotho has made important progress in improving access to electricity through its Clean Energy Access Transformation initiative, which has now benefited more than 147,000 people across the country. T...

item.title: Australian shares edge lower as oil prices rise and DroneShield faces shareholder backlash
item.description: Australian shares finished lower on Monday as investors monitored developments surrounding a potential long-term ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. Ongoing uncertainty over the St...

item.title: Key outcomes and controversies from the first week of Senate estimates hearings
item.description: The first week of Australia’s Senate estimates hearings has revealed significant developments across the public service, including workforce reductions, funding pressures, accountability concerns, and...

item.title: Rural Eastern Cape still reflects apartheid spatial neglect, argues Lehohla
item.description: Pali Lehohla argues that the enduring poverty in Xolobe and Qutubeni, the rural birthplaces of Albertina and Walter Sisulu, reflects the unfinished business of South Africa’s democratic transition. Ra...

item.title: Karachi Traffic Police implement e-challan system for lane discipline on Sharea Faisal
item.description: Starting June 1, 2026, the Karachi Traffic Police have officially activated the e-challan system to monitor and penalise lane violations on Sharea Faisal under the Traffic Regulation and Citation Syst...

item.title: Full list of films and TV series leaving Netflix UK and US in June 2026
item.description: Netflix subscribers in the UK and US face a significant content purge in June 2026, with a wide range of films and television series scheduled to leave the platform throughout the month. The monthly r...

item.title: Los Angeles Officials Reaffirm Availability of In-Person Voting Ahead of Primary Election
item.description: In the days leading up to California’s June 2 primary election, former President Donald Trump made inaccurate claims suggesting that Los Angeles conducts elections exclusively by mail and lacks in-per...

item.title: Governance failures across Australian universities draw increasing scrutiny amid leadership controversies
item.description: Education commentator Julie Hare argues that governance problems are affecting a significant number of Australian universities, extending well beyond the highly publicised issues at the Australian Nat...

item.title: Joe Sibanyoni withdraws court application as extortion case proceeds
item.description: Mpumalanga taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni, also known as Joe “Ferrari”, and his co-accused have formally withdrawn their urgent High Court application that sought to stop the re-enrolment of their extortion ...

item.title: Cute Abiola apologises to Patience Jonathan over past ridicule of her emotional reactions to insecurity
item.description: Nigerian skit maker Cute Abiola don publicly apologise to former First Lady Patience Jonathan over how Nigerians, including himself, mocked her emotional reactions in the past when she dey speak about...

item.title: Domeng weakens as it moves north, but monsoon rains continue across Luzon and nearby areas
item.description: Typhoon Domeng (international name Jangmi) has slightly weakened while moving northward over the Philippine Sea, according to PAGASA. As of 11 a.m. on June 1, the storm was located about 710 kilometer...

item.title: UK and France intercept sanctioned Russian oil tanker in Atlantic joint operation
item.description: France and the United Kingdom have taken part in a joint maritime operation to intercept a tanker suspected of violating international sanctions on Russia, according to French President Emmanuel Macro...

item.title: Los Angeles mayoral candidates campaign across the city ahead of primary election
item.description: In the final stretch before Los Angeles’ primary election, the leading mayoral candidates—incumbent Karen Bass, City Councilmember Nithya Raman, and former reality TV personality Spencer Pratt—spent t...

item.title: KZN road safety operation arrests 13 motorists for drunk driving, including taxi driver 20 times over limit and SANDF member
item.description: A KwaZulu-Natal road safety enforcement operation in Ladysmith has resulted in the arrest of 13 motorists for driving under the influence of alcohol, including a taxi driver found to be 20 times over ...

item.title: ODM-aligned Linda Mwananchi faction plans major political rally in Thika to expand Central Kenya outreach
item.description: The ODM-aligned Linda Mwananchi faction has announced plans to hold a major political rally in Thika on 14 June as part of its broader nationwide mobilisation campaign ahead of Kenya’s 2027 General El...

item.title: Employment tribunal awards £149,000 to athlete after findings of unfair treatment and pay disputes at Nuffield Health
item.description: A former canoe polo champion and personal trainer, Beth Littlewood from Bridgend, has been awarded around £149,000 in compensation following a series of employment tribunal findings against Nuffield H...

item.title: India’s AI transition and the need to prioritise human capital for employment growth
item.description: The article discusses concerns around the growing impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on global employment, warning of what some economists describe as an “AI jobs apocalypse.” It argues that unlik...

item.title: Minister Aucamp Highlights Centenary and Future Stewardship of Kruger National Park
item.description: Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Willie Aucamp, addressed the centenary celebrations of Kruger National Park (KNP) at Skukuza Rest Camp in Mpumalanga, urging South Africans to embr...
